Title: Characterisation of Early Biosynthetic Steps of Atractylon via an Integrative Strategy

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early atractylon branch via germacrane sesquiterpenoid intermediate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms an important intermediate/precursor pool for atractylon biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes FPP to produce mainly germacrene B and minor sesquiterpenes including β-elemene, caryophyllene and humulene
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control enzyme essential in the initial/early biosynthetic steps of atractylon, but not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive medicinal sesquiterpenoid of A. lancea
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased germacrene B, β-elemene and atractylon; silencing decreased all three, demonstrating positive control of atractylon accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in root and rhizome; much lower in group blue than in group red and green
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Preferentially expressed in root and rhizome, where atractylon accumulates
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: mGWAS performed after all accessions were grown in the same garden for 1 year to minimize environmental effects
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS; RNA-seq expression profiling; in vitro enzyme assay with FPP and GC-MS; transgenic root overexpression; VIGS silencing in rhizomes; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequencing, resequencing, RNA-seq, metabolomics/GC-MS; CNGBdb: CNP0006702
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for breeding and metabolic engineering to enhance atractylon production; can be prioritized for selection or overexpression in medicinal germplasm
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: mGWAS identified a significant region associated with atractylon accumulation; AlTPSa47 orthologs show conserved synteny across Asteraceae; mutational analysis identified residues essential for activity; germacrene B was proposed as an important atractylon intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AlTPSa47 was identified by mGWAS and tissue expression analysis as the strongest biosynthetic candidate associated with atractylon variation. 2. Recombinant AlTPSa47 converted FPP mainly to germacrene B, with β-elemene, caryophyllene and humulene as minor products. 3. Overexpression in A. lancea transgenic roots increased β-elemene, germacrene B and atractylon, while VIGS reduced them, confirming AlTPSa47 functions in early atractylon biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase family, TPS-a subfamily, Clade 2-1
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms branch sesquiterpenoid products contributing to sesquiterpenoid diversity rather than directly validated for atractylon
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes FPP to three isomeric bisabolene-related sesquiterpenoids: γ-curcumene, zingiberene and β-sesquiphellandrene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to volatile/medicinal sesquiterpenoid diversity in A. lancea
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces multiple bisabolene-type sesquiterpenoids in vitro, supporting contribution to sesquiterpenoid diversity
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher expression in both rhizome and root
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher expression in rhizome and root
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; recombinant protein enzyme assay with FPP; GC-MS product identification; RNA-seq expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering sesquiterpenoid composition/diversity in A. lancea
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phylogenetically grouped with Clade 2-1 enzymes associated with bisabolene sesquiterpenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AlTPSa07 is a cloned TPS-a enzyme with notable rhizome/root expression. 2. In vitro it converts FPP into γ-curcumene, zingiberene and β-sesquiphellandrene. 3. It likely contributes to the bisabolene-type sesquiterpenoid branch in A. lancea.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase family, TPS-a subfamily, Clade 2-2
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Generates diverse sesquiterpene skeletons contributing to overall sesquiterpenoid diversity
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes FPP to multiple sesquiterpenes including tricyclic products silphinene, modephene, α-isocomene and β-isocomene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces multiple structurally distinct sesquiterpenoids, indicating contribution to chemical diversity among A. lancea groups
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression pattern differed among groups; higher in group blue and red than green
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; in vitro enzyme assay with FPP; GC-MS; RNA-seq expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to diversify sesquiterpene composition in breeding or synthetic biology
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified in Clade 2-2, which the authors infer contributes to Asteraceae sesquiterpenoid diversity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AlTPSa51 is a functionally active TPS-a enzyme from Clade 2-2. 2. It produces four sesquiterpenoids, including tricyclic compounds. 3. Its group-dependent expression suggests a role in sesquiterpenoid divergence among A. lancea lineages.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase family, TPS-a subfamily, Clade 2-2
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Produces a specific sesquiterpenoid associated with group-specific metabolite accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts FPP to a single detected product, cyclohexanemethanol (12), in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Its product cyclohexanemethanol accumulated more highly in group blue, consistent with higher AlTPSa53 expression in group blue
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in group blue than other groups
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; in vitro enzyme assay with FPP; GC-MS; RNA-seq expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be used to alter group-specific volatile sesquiterpene composition
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors link its expression pattern with higher accumulation of product 12 in group blue
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AlTPSa53 is a Clade 2-2 TPS-a enzyme with a single detected in vitro product. 2. It produces cyclohexanemethanol (12) from FPP. 3. Higher expression in group blue matches higher abundance of product 12 in that group.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase family, TPS-a subfamily, Clade 2-2
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Produces branch sesquiterpenes contributing to total volatile sesquiterpenoid pool
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes FPP to caryophyllene as the major product and humulene as a minor product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates caryophyllene and humulene in vitro, helping explain why these minor AlTPSa47 products were not group-specific
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; in vitro enzyme assay with FPP; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tool for tuning caryophyllene/humulene content
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors note caryophyllene and humulene can be synthesized by enzymes other than AlTPSa47, including AlTPSa56
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AlTPSa56 is a functional TPS-a enzyme from Clade 2-2. 2. It converts FPP mainly to caryophyllene and minor humulene. 3. It contributes to the broader sesquiterpene product spectrum in A. lancea.
